description:
Advances: specled.spec_review (and possibly specled.realized_by — if a new detector_unavailable reason is added)

A new affected subject with no realized_by declared looks identical in the report to a subject whose bindings simply weren't touched. The CODE diagram node tooltip (lib/specled_ex/review/html.ex:296) says "0 MFAs means no realized_by — neither pass nor fail" but that's tooltip-buried.

Two options, pick one:

A. Emit a detector_unavailable finding with reason :no_realized_by from the realization pipeline when a subject has zero bindings. Then child #2's :degraded leg state surfaces it automatically.

B. Inline advisory on the subject card header: "this subject has no realized_by binding declared — SPEC↔CODE leg cannot be verified."

Option A is more aligned with concepts.md's graceful-degrade rule (detector_unavailable is the right vocabulary for "this tier cannot run on this subject"). Option B is local-only.

Depends on child #2.

comments.text: MERGED into specled_-qm1/spec-review-html. Closing per orchestrator.

title: No-realized_by advisory on subject cards
